text.title: competitive-intelligence Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for competitive-intelligence as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Mixing "no_decision" outcomes with competitive losses in win-rate calculations
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Inflates the denominator of competitive loss rate; stalled-pipeline rate requires different treatment and different corrective action than competitive displacement.

failure-mode.description: Using only rep notes without any buyer-side quote for loss coding
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Rep notes systematically over-attribute losses to "price" and "competition" to avoid admitting process or product failures that reflect on rep performance.

failure-mode.description: Reporting a single median price instead of a range (P25/P50/P75)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Single medians create false precision. The spread between P25 and P75 is often the strategic signal — it shows whether the market has standardized pricing or wide negotiation room.

failure-mode.description: Anchoring on the loudest competitor in review-site analysis
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Market-share leaders dominate review-site volume; emerging threats are invisible until they take a deal. Stratify by segment and deal-size, not raw mention count.

failure-mode.description: Citing the Crayon "16% to 45% win rate" case study without attribution control
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: That uplift conflates CI rollout with new pricing, new battlecards, and new sales hires. Attributing it solely to CI misleads leadership about expected ROI.

failure-mode.description: Agent invents a loss reason from rep notes without a buyer-side quote
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force the output schema to require at least 1 verbatim quote with a speaker field; reject rows where evidence_quotes is empty.

failure-mode.description: Agent reports "no Enterprise tier exists" when competitor hides it behind "Contact Sales"
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mitigate with G2-disclosed price ranges and LinkedIn job posts mentioning ACV bands. Absence of a public pricing page does not mean absence of an Enterprise tier.

failure-mode.description: Agent defends sales rep narrative ("buyer was confused about the pricing")
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use adversarial prompt framing: "Argue the buyer was right to choose the competitor; cite evidence." This surfaces the buyer-side perspective the rep omits.

failure-mode.description: Agent paraphrases buyer quotes, losing attribution
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Preserve speaker_role, company_size, and segment end-to-end, never the buyer's name. Paraphrased quotes are useless to leadership — they cannot be cited or challenged.

failure-mode.description: Agent adds new loss-reason categories each run, breaking trend lines
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin the loss-reason enum in the output schema; flag deals that do not fit for human triage rather than silently expanding the taxonomy.
